Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for auto-categorizer/expense-related issues using linear_list_issues. Discover ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-212, ENG-219, ENG-220 and their current states.
OE 2: Search Slack via conversations_search_messages for discussions confirming actual completion of work items - particularly Lena's deployment confirmation, Dmitri's audit results, Anh's dashboard validation status, and Samira's remediation plan. Default tool, but the agent must construct non-obvious queries to find cross-referencing evidence.
OE 3: Search emails via search_emails for Samira <-> Elena audit results correspondence and remediation plan.
OE 4: Search Airtable stipend records via airtable_list_records to find incorrectly auto-decided expense records - both Mosaic false approvals ("Approved" status) and Vectral false rejections ("Rejected" status).
OE 5: Look up email addresses for Elena Rostova, Lena Bjorkstrom, Anh Nguyen, Dmitri Volkov via contacts_search_contacts.
OE 6: Close ENG-214 via linear_update_issue with state set to "Done" and a note summarizing the verified deployment.
OE 7: Close ENG-215 via linear_update_issue with state set to "Done" and a note summarizing the verified deployment.
OE 8: Close ENG-216 via linear_update_issue with state set to "Done" and a note summarizing the completed audit.
OE 9: Contact Lena Bjorkstrom about ENG-217 blocker via conversations_add_message or linear_create_comment.
OE 10: Contact Anh Nguyen about ENG-218 blocker via conversations_add_message or linear_create_comment.
OE 11: Contact Dmitri Volkov about ENG-212 remaining work via conversations_add_message or linear_create_comment.
OE 12: Update Mosaic Robotics false approval records via airtable_update_records - change Approval Status from "Approved" to "Rejected" for records recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023, rec_stipend_andre_desk, rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork.
OE 13: Update Vectral Systems false rejection records via airtable_update_records - change Approval Status from "Rejected" to "Approved" for records recStip000000018 (Maria Santos $550), recStip000000019 (James Okafor $82), recStip000000020 (Lisa Park $130).
OE 14: Update ENG-219 launch readiness checklist via linear_update_issue with current gate-by-gate status.
OE 15: Post status summary to #engineering (channel C003) via conversations_add_message.
OE 16: Send relaunch timeline email to Elena Rostova via send_email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

9 The message to Dmitri (visible in conversations_add_message or linear_create_comment parameters) asks about the remaining work on ENG-212 edge case tests - specifically categorization edge cases, vendor abbreviation handling, or internet setup/monthly distinction.
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1The #engineering message mentions ENG-212 as 'Edge case tests' stalled but does not specifically reference the remaining categorization edge cases, vendor abbreviation handling, or internet setup/monthly distinction. It's a generic status request, not specific about what sub-work remains.
✗Run #2The message to Dmitri about ENG-212 mentions 'Schema validation tests, malformed data handling, config-vs-source' but does not specifically reference categorization edge cases, vendor abbreviation handling, or internet setup/monthly distinction as required by the criterion. These specific items appeared in the ENG-212 ticket but were not surfaced in the outreach message.
✓Run #3The #engineering Slack message explicitly mentions 'ENG-212: Edge case tests (@Dmitri) — Schema validation tests are confirmed from the audit, but what about the remaining items (category overlap, bundled items, vendor abbreviations)?' — specifically calls out categorization edge cases including vendor abbreviations.
✗Run #4The message to Dmitri asks '@Dmitri — Can you start ENG-220 Layer 1 this week?' — about ENG-220 (duplicate detection), not ENG-212 edge case tests. The agent incorrectly marked ENG-212 as Done without checking on the remaining categorization edge cases (vendor abbreviation handling, internet setup/monthly distinction).
✗Run #5The message to Dmitri/ENG-212 asks generically whether 'the formal test suite complete and merged' but does not mention specific categorization edge cases, vendor abbreviation handling, or internet setup/monthly distinction. The message lacks the required specificity about remaining categorization work.
✓Run #6The #engineering message asks Dmitri: 'Is the full suite done (malformed JSON handling, config-vs-source diff, category overlap)? Or did this pause when you pivoted to ENG-220 scoping?' — specifically referencing the remaining categorization edge cases in ENG-212.
10 At least one Mosaic Robotics expense record that was incorrectly auto-approved (one of: recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023, rec_stipend_andre_desk, or rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork) was updated via airtable_update_records with Approval Status changed from "Approved" to "Rejected", and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never updated any Mosaic Robotics false approval records. It only corrected Vectral false rejection records (18, 19, 20, rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi). No airtable_update_records call targeted Mosaic records to change status from Approved to Rejected.
✗Run #2The agent never updated any Mosaic Robotics false-approval records from 'Approved' to 'Rejected'. Both airtable_update_records calls only corrected Vectral false-rejection records (recStip000000018/19/20 and rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk/rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi), all changed to Approved. No Mosaic records were touched.
✗Run #3The agent did NOT update any Mosaic Robotics expense records from Approved to Rejected. The two airtable_update_records calls only corrected Vectral false rejections (records 18, 19, 20) to Approved and updated duplicate Vectral records. The agent explicitly noted 'Mosaic false approvals absorbed per Elena's directive' and left them as Approved, despite the criterion requiring that incorrectly auto-approved Mosaic expenses be corrected to Rejected.
✗Run #4The agent never called airtable_update_records on any Mosaic records (recStip000000021/22/23 or rec_stipend_andre_desk/kenji). The email states these were left 'as-is per your directive to absorb the costs,' but Elena's directive to absorb costs does not mean the system status should remain 'Approved' for policy-violating expenses. No Mosaic correction was made.
✗Run #5The agent did not update any Mosaic expense records from 'Approved' to 'Rejected'. Both airtable_update_records calls only corrected Vectral false-denial records (recStip000000018, 19, 20 and rec_stipend_maria_santos_desk, rec_stipend_lisa_park_wifi). The Mosaic false-approval records were not modified in Airtable.
✗Run #6The agent did not update any Mosaic false approval records (recStip000000021, recStip000000022, recStip000000023, rec_stipend_andre_desk, or rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork) from 'Approved' to 'Rejected'. The agent explicitly stated in the email that 'The 3 Mosaic false approvals...are documented and being absorbed per your directive,' leaving them at Approved status without correcting the system records.
11 At least one Vectral Systems expense record that was incorrectly auto-rejected (one of: recStip000000018 for Maria Santos $550, recStip000000019 for James Okafor $82, or recStip000000020 for Lisa Park $130) was updated via airtable_update_records with Approval Status changed from "Rejected" to "Approved," and the tool returned success.

✓Run #4The email to Elena explicitly states 'Week of May 11: Relaunch (earliest realistic date)' and identifies multiple blockers (ENG-217 stalled, ENG-218 unknown, ENG-220 not started, shadow mode not run), making clear the auto-categorizer is not ready for immediate relaunch.
